req.title: Create encounter
req.statement: A clinician shall create an Encounter for a patient with start and end timestamps; end before start shall be rejected.

doc.reasoning: The requirement has two clear decision branches: (1) valid start < end produces a stored encounter, (2) end < start produces HTTP 422. BVA drives a boundary case where end equals start (same instant), which sits on the partition boundary and tests whether the system treats equal timestamps as valid or invalid. EP covers the happy path with a clearly valid interval. NEG covers the inverted interval. DT would capture combined field combinations; STC is less applicable here since the requirement is stateless from the test perspective. Each test uses a fixture for auth headers and a helper to create a transient patient, ensuring idempotency via unique resource identifiers per run.

tests.name: test_create_encounter_valid_interval_returns_201
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a clinician (role:clinician)
A patient resource exists in the system
actions: POST /Encounter with start='2024-06-01T08:00:00Z' and end='2024-06-01T09:00:00Z' for the existing patient
expected: Response body contains an Encounter resource with a server-assigned id
Stored start and end timestamps match the submitted values
trace: R-HLT-006: start < end is the valid equivalence class
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from uuid import uuid4

def test_create_encounter_valid_interval_returns_201(clinician_headers, existing_patient_id, base_url):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter",
        "status": "finished",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{existing_patient_id}"},
        "period": {
            "start": "2024-06-01T08:00:00Z",
            "end": "2024-06-01T09:00:00Z"
        },
        "identifier": [{"value": str(uuid4())}]
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Encounter",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Encounter"
    assert "id" in body
    assert body["period"]["start"] == "2024-06-01T08:00:00Z"
    assert body["period"]["end"] == "2024-06-01T09:00:00Z"

tests.name: test_create_encounter_end_before_start_returns_422
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a clinician (role:clinician)
A patient resource exists in the system
actions: POST /Encounter with start='2024-06-01T09:00:00Z' and end='2024-06-01T08:00:00Z' (end strictly before start)
expected: Response body contains an error indicating invalid period (end before start)
trace: R-HLT-006: error pathway — 422 when end < start
R-HLT-006: encounter must NOT be stored
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from uuid import uuid4

def test_create_encounter_end_before_start_returns_422(clinician_headers, existing_patient_id, base_url):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter",
        "status": "finished",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{existing_patient_id}"},
        "period": {
            "start": "2024-06-01T09:00:00Z",
            "end": "2024-06-01T08:00:00Z"
        },
        "identifier": [{"value": str(uuid4())}]
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Encounter",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body is not None, "Expected non-empty error body"

tests.name: test_create_encounter_end_equals_start_boundary
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a clinician (role:clinician)
A patient resource exists in the system
actions: POST /Encounter with start='2024-06-01T08:00:00Z' and end='2024-06-01T08:00:00Z' (end exactly equals start — zero-duration encounter)
expected: HTTP status is either 201 (if zero-duration is accepted) or 422 (if zero-duration is rejected)
Response is deterministic and consistent with the server's boundary policy
If 201: resource is stored with matching timestamps
If 422: error body indicates invalid period
trace: R-HLT-006: BVA on the start==end boundary of the end>=start / end<start partition
R-HLT-006: clarifies whether the boundary is inclusive or exclusive
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from uuid import uuid4

ACCEPTED_STATUSES = {201, 422}

def test_create_encounter_end_equals_start_boundary(clinician_headers, existing_patient_id, base_url):
    ts = "2024-06-01T08:00:00Z"
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter",
        "status": "finished",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{existing_patient_id}"},
        "period": {"start": ts, "end": ts},
        "identifier": [{"value": str(uuid4())}]
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Encounter",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in ACCEPTED_STATUSES, (
        f"Expected 201 or 422 at boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    if resp.status_code == 201:
        assert body.get("period", {}).get("start") == ts
        assert body.get("period", {}).get("end") == ts
    else:
        assert body is not None, "Expected non-empty error body on 422"

tests.name: test_create_encounter_transitions_patient_to_active_encounter_state
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a clinician (role:clinician)
A patient exists with no current in-progress encounter
actions: POST /Encounter with status='in-progress', start='2024-06-01T10:00:00Z', no end timestamp
expected: GET returns the newly created in-progress encounter for the patient
Patient is now in the 'encounter active' state
trace: STC: patient state transitions from no-encounter to encounter-active
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from uuid import uuid4

def test_create_encounter_transitions_patient_to_active_encounter_state(clinician_headers, existing_patient_id, base_url):
    uid = str(uuid4())
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter",
        "status": "in-progress",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{existing_patient_id}"},
        "period": {"start": "2024-06-01T10:00:00Z"},
        "identifier": [{"value": uid}]
    }
    post_resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Encounter",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_headers
    )
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {post_resp.status_code}: {post_resp.text}"
    encounter_id = post_resp.json()["id"]
    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/Encounter",
        params={"patient": existing_patient_id, "status": "in-progress"},
        headers=clinician_headers
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    ids = [e["id"] for e in get_resp.json().get("entry", [])]
    assert encounter_id in ids, f"Encounter {encounter_id} not found in active encounters"

tests.name: test_create_encounter_decision_table_missing_start_returns_error
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a clinician (role:clinician)
A patient resource exists in the system
actions: POST /Encounter with end timestamp provided but start timestamp omitted (invalid combination per decision table)
expected: HTTP 400 or 422 — start is a required field; request is rejected
Response body contains a field-level validation error referencing period.start
trace: R-HLT-006: start timestamp is required for encounter creation
DT: decision table row — start=absent, end=present → invalid input → error response
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from uuid import uuid4

REJECT_STATUSES = {400, 422}

def test_create_encounter_decision_table_missing_start_returns_error(clinician_headers, existing_patient_id, base_url):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter",
        "status": "finished",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{existing_patient_id}"},
        "period": {"end": "2024-06-01T09:00:00Z"},
        "identifier": [{"value": str(uuid4())}]
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Encounter",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in REJECT_STATUSES, (
        f"Expected 400 or 422 for missing start, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert body is not None, "Expected non-empty error body"
